methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $129M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

data.narrative:
INERATEC, a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Tim Boeltken, has raised $129M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five same-stage, same-sector private companies (Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, CarbonCapture Inc., and Cemvita) alongside one public comparable, with a total comparable universe of nine market participants.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $129M total raised proxy, yielding $245M. The peer-median method anchors to the median valuation of five same-sector Series B companies at $310M, with a range spanning $120M (Cemvita) to $500M (Pano AI). The peer set exhibits material dispersion, reflecting heterogeneous risk and commercialization profiles within frontier climate tech.

Triangulation across both methods produces a median valuation of $278M, with a low-end estimate of $208M and a high-end estimate of $357M. The gap between stage-progression ($245M) and peer-median ($310M) reflects typical variation in how recent funding and peer valuations map onto post-money value. Key uncertainties include the target's technology maturity relative to higher-valued peers and the sustainability of recent comparable valuations in current market conditions. The $278M median is most defensible for near-term financing contexts but should be stress-tested against technology-specific risks and capital efficiency metrics.
